reasoning: The first payment of $3.1M was completed around April 9, 2025, specifically covering 378 out of 740 farms for which compensation had already been approved. This strongly suggests that payments are being processed in batches or on a rolling basis, making it very likely that the remaining ~362 approved farms will receive their initial 1% cash payment soon. Furthermore, the government has high political and economic motivation (specifically to appease international lenders like the IMF/World Bank for its $21 billion debt restructuring) to demonstrate consistent momentum in honoring this deal. Given the remaining budget allocation ($20 million in the 2025 budget) and active processing of the currently approved roster, further disbursements are probable within the next ~5 weeks. However, Zimbabwe's severe liquidity constraints and typical bureaucratic delays prevent my confidence from being extremely high in such a short window, keeping the estimate at a measured 68%.
batch_queries: Will the Zimbabwe, before June 1, 2025, make another payment to white farmers whose property was seized?
